summary: The reproduction bundle cloned Langflow v1.7.0, started the real app in-process, invoked the unauthenticated auto_login route, and verified that the returned token authenticated as the default superuser.
expected_security_impact: The helper should obtain a bearer token from GET /api/v1/auto_login, resolve /api/v1/users/whoami to the default langflow superuser, access the superuser-only GET /api/v1/users/ route, and emit EVOHUNT_AUTH_POC_SUCCESS.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned Langflow v1.7.0 at commit b6ed2bc4d9d6a00e4bd8363495edfec6f091ccfc, built the reproduction image, obtained a bearer token from GET /api/v1/auto_login, resolved /api/v1/users/whoami to username langflow with is_superuser=true, accessed GET /api/v1/users/ with HTTP 200, and printed EVOHUNT_AUTH_POC_SUCCESS.
verification_method: The container helper fails unless GET /api/v1/auto_login returns 200, the returned token resolves to username langflow with is_superuser=true, and the same token receives 200 from GET /api/v1/users/. On success it prints the exact proof string EVOHUNT_AUTH_POC_SUCCESS.
evidence_produced_by_run: run.sh observed the expected proof signal EVOHUNT_AUTH_POC_SUCCESS.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, stages the required source tree into the Docker build context, and mounts the checked-out repository into the container at /target-repo.
